summary: Pydantic models provide basic type validation. No field-length limits, allowlist validation, or sanitization on user-facing string inputs.

tests.description: No URL length limit — accepts arbitrarily long strings
tests.actual: URLRequest.url = str — no length, format, or scheme validation at Pydantic level
tests.evidence: routes/url.py line 13-14: class URLRequest(BaseModel): url: str
tests.notes: analyze_url() adds https:// prefix if missing and parses with urlparse — but no upper-bound on input length. A 10MB string payload would reach the analyzer.

tests.description: OTP message truncated to 200 chars in DB but full string passed to analyzer
tests.actual: analyze_otp(request.message) receives full unbounded message; only DB write uses [:200] slice
tests.evidence: routes/otp.py line 18: result = analyze_otp(request.message) — unbounded; line 24: input_data=request.message[:200] — only DB is bounded
tests.notes: If the OTP analyzer uses regex patterns over a large input, this could cause ReDoS with crafted payloads.

tests.description: No file-size limit on base64 image payload — arbitrary large payloads accepted
tests.actual: ScreenshotRequest.image = str — no size cap. A 50MB base64 payload will be decoded and processed.
tests.evidence: routes/screenshot.py line 13-14: class ScreenshotRequest(BaseModel): image: str
tests.category: Missing Input Validation / DoS via Large Payload
tests.notes: The analyzer decodes base64, opens PIL Image, and optionally sends to OpenAI API. A crafted gigantic payload could exhaust memory or consume expensive API credits.

tests.description: No audio size limit and `format` field not allowlisted to mp3/m4a
tests.expected: format validated to ['mp3','m4a']; audio size bounded
tests.actual: VoiceRequest.format = str with default 'mp3' — any arbitrary string accepted. audio = str — unbounded.
tests.evidence: routes/voice.py line 14-16: class VoiceRequest(BaseModel): audio: str; format: str = 'mp3'
tests.notes: The format value is likely passed downstream to OpenAI Whisper API. An unexpected format string could cause unexpected API behavior or errors. Additionally, a massive base64 audio blob could exhaust OpenAI API credits.

tests.description: UPI ID has no format validation — arbitrary strings accepted
tests.expected: UPI ID validated against standard format: [a-z0-9.\-_]{2,256}@[a-z]{2,64}
tests.actual: UPIRequest.upi_id = str — no regex or length constraint
tests.notes: Low risk due to ORM-based DB writes, but malformed UPI IDs may cause unexpected analyzer behavior.

tests.description: FastAPI default behavior — invalid JSON body returns 422 Unprocessable Entity with detailed error schema
tests.actual: Pydantic model validation correctly rejects missing/wrong-type fields with 422
tests.notes: Basic type validation is in place. The concern is the LACK of length/format/allowlist constraints on string fields.

tests.description: URL field is used to parse and analyze — check for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) potential
tests.expected: URL scheme and host validated / blocklisted for internal ranges
tests.actual: analyze_url() uses urlparse but performs feature extraction only — no outbound HTTP requests made to the URL itself in url_analyzer.py (static analysis only). SSRF not present.
tests.evidence: services/url_analyzer.py: no requests.get() or httpx calls to the submitted URL — analysis is purely heuristic/regex/ML
tests.notes: SSRF is NOT present in url_analyzer.py as of current codebase. If future feature to 'visit URL to check content' is added, SSRF protection must be implemented first.
